regions.name: Medellín
regions.stay_type: design hotel in El Poblado or Laureles

activities.category: City transformation story & urban design walk
Local market and street food circuit
Day trip to Guatapé and El Peñol

regions.name: Coffee Region (Eje Cafetero)
regions.stay_type: working coffee hacienda with farm access

activities.category: Seed-to-cup coffee harvest experience
Wax palm forest hike in Valle del Cocora
Salento colonial town wander

regions.name: Cartagena
regions.stay_type: colonial-house boutique in the walled city

activities.category: Walled city history walk at dusk
Day trip to Islas del Rosario
Rooftop sunset with Caribbean coast context

common_mistakes: Staying in El Centro in Medellín — it's fine for day visits but El Poblado or Laureles are where you want to base yourself
Doing the Coffee Region as a day trip from Medellín — three nights minimum to actually absorb the rhythm of hacienda life
Visiting Cartagena in July or August when cruise ship traffic peaks and prices spike 40–60%
Flying between all three legs — the Medellín-to-coffee-region drive is scenic and only two hours; save flights for the final Cartagena leg

regions.name: Bogotá
regions.stay_type: boutique hotel in La Candelaria or Chapinero

activities.category: Gold Museum and pre-Columbian history
Monserrate sunrise hike
Ciclovía and local Sunday culture

regions.name: Amazon (Leticia region)
regions.stay_type: jungle lodge reachable only by boat

activities.category: Dawn birdwatching with indigenous guide
Piranha fishing and river ecology talk
Village visit and plant medicine walk

regions.name: Coffee Region (Eje Cafetero)
regions.stay_type: working coffee hacienda with farm access

activities.category: Seed-to-cup coffee harvest experience
Wax palm forest hike in Valle del Cocora

regions.name: Tayrona National Park coast
regions.stay_type: eco-camp or tented lodge within park boundaries

activities.category: Lost City (Ciudad Perdida) trekking — multi-day option
Jungle-to-beach hike on Tayrona trails
Indigenous Kogi community interaction

common_mistakes: Flying to Leticia without at least 3 nights — you can't see the real Amazon in a 48-hour visit
Booking Tayrona accommodation through generic sites — the park-approved eco-camps sell out months ahead and can't be found on booking platforms
Attempting the Lost City trek without proper fitness preparation — four days of humid jungle hiking at altitude is not a casual walk
Scheduling the Amazon leg in February or March when the river is at its lowest and wildlife disperses

regions.name: Santa Marta
regions.stay_type: boutique hotel in the historic centre

activities.category: Arrival orientation walk through the colonial centre
Evening seafood dinner on the waterfront
Dawn birdwatching in the Sierra Nevada cloud forest
Coffee and cacao farm tour — seed to cup and bean to bar
Waterfall hike to Pozo Azul or La Victoria

regions.name: Parque Tayrona
regions.stay_type: eco-camp within the park — tents or hammocks at the jungle edge

activities.category: Jungle trail hike to Cabo San Juan and Arrecifes beach
Sunrise swim at a protected Caribbean cove
Biodiversity spotting walk with a park naturalist

regions.name: Palomino
regions.stay_type: beachfront eco-hostel or bohemian riverside lodge

activities.category: Río Palomino tubing — guided float from jungle to sea
Visit to a Kogui indigenous community in the Sierra Nevada foothills
Yoga or breathwork session at a beachfront shala

common_mistakes: Visiting Tayrona without advance eco-camp bookings — the park-approved sites fill up months ahead and can't be found on standard booking platforms
Skipping Santa Marta as 'just a hub' — the historic centre has one excellent evening before heading into the sierra
Planning Tayrona in February or August when the park closes for its periodic ecological rest — check the closure calendar before booking
Underestimating the Minca-to-Palomino road — it's scenic but unpaved in sections; book a trusted driver, not a generic taxi

regions.name: Medellín & Guatapé
regions.stay_type: design hotel in El Poblado or Laureles

activities.category: City transformation story and urban design walk
Day trip to Guatapé — Piedra del Peñol rock climb and lake circuit
Medellín nightlife and gastronomy circuit in El Poblado

regions.name: Jardín
regions.stay_type: family-run posada in the colonial centre

activities.category: Coffee farm visit — less commercial than Eje Cafetero, more authentic
Guadua cable car over the valley and canyon hike
Dawn birdwatching in the Andean cloud forest around the town

regions.name: Eje Cafetero (Salento, Filandia & Valle del Cocora)
regions.stay_type: working coffee hacienda with farm access

activities.category: Seed-to-cup coffee harvest experience at a family finca
Wax palm forest hike in Valle del Cocora — Colombia's national tree
Salento and Filandia colonial town photography walk

common_mistakes: Treating Jardín as a day trip from Medellín — two nights minimum to feel the town's rhythm and reach the best coffee farms
Visiting Eje Cafetero on a weekend in high season without booking hacienda accommodation months ahead
Skipping Filandia in favour of Salento alone — Filandia is less crowded and just as photogenic for the coloured facades
Not bringing a rain layer for Valle del Cocora — the cloud forest earns its name and afternoon mist is the norm even in dry season
